query: 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis latest status July 2026 - are attacks on ships continuing or has there been a ceasefire/truce? UKMTO attack incidents in Persian Gulf, Strait of Hormuz, Gulf of Oman in July 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here is my summary of the key findings:

## Summary: 2026 Strait of Hormuz Crisis — Status as of July 13, 2026

**Bottom line: The crisis is ESCALATING, not in ceasefire or de-escalation.**

**Are attacks continuing?** Yes. After the July 6–7 attacks on three tankers, Iran's IRGC struck a Cyprus-flagged container ship on July 11 and **formally declared the Strait of Hormuz closed**. The US disputes this, insisting the strait is open.

**Ceasefire/truce status:** Collapsed. The June 17 US–Iran MoU and its 60-day negotiating window have functionally failed; Oman-mediated talks broke down by July 13. The US and Iran are exchanging direct military strikes for a second consecutive night.

**Ship attack incidents (June–July 2026):**
- **June 25/27:** M/T Kiku (Panama tanker) — drone strikes
- **July 6–7 ("three ships in 24 hrs"):** Al Rekayyat (Qatari LNG), Wedyan (Saudi tanker), Cyprus Prosperity (Liberian tanker) — UKMTO reported a tanker struck by a projectile causing fire off Limah, Oman
- **July 11:** Cyprus-flagged container ship struck and set ablaze (23 crew, 1 missing) — triggered Iran's closure declaration
- **UANI total:** ~52 commercial-vessel maritime incidents across the Gulf/Hormuz/Gulf of Oman since Feb 28

**Military escalation:** US hit 140 Iranian targets July 11, "dozens" July 12 (first US use of sea attack drones). Iran retaliated against US bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar; Jordan intercepted 4 missiles.

**Traffic & markets:** Transits collapsed to ~21 on July 11 (vs. 120–140/day pre-war). Brent crude rose to ~$78.70 on July 13 — up but well below the ~$126 spring peak.

**No mine-clearance lull:** Early-July demining progress on the southern corridor was overtaken by the closure and renewed strikes.

**Key caveat:** CNN referenced an "Indian container ship" on July 11 while Time described a "Cyprus-flagged container ship" — likely the same vessel (Cyprus flag, Indian crew), but not fully reconciled across sources.

Note: AskNews tools were unavailable (credit limit), so I relied on web search, Wikipedia, CNN, Al Jazeera, Time, CNBC, UANI, and Windward.
